data.title: Japan's CPI surpass expectations; annual inflation hits 2-year high to 4%
data.description:
Data showed core inflation in Japan rose to 3.2% in January, up from 3% in December, exceeding forecasts of 3.1%. Headline inflation also increased to 4%, up from 3.6%, marking the highest level in two years.

The January figure also represents the highest level since June 2023, further solidifying a hawkish outlook for the Bank of Japan's monetary policy.

The core inflation rate rose to a 19-month high of 3.2%, up from 3.0% in December and topping consensus of 3.1%.

Monthly, the CPI increased by 0.5%, after December's 14-month top of 0.6% rise.

Food prices in Japan increased by 7.8% from a year earlier in January 2025, accelerating from a 6.4% rise in the previous month. It was the highest food inflation since October 2023, with upward price pressures.

Separately, the Au Jibun Bank Japan Manufacturing PMI inched higher to 48.9 in February 2025 from January’s ten-month low of 48.7, compared to market estimates of 49, preliminary estimates showed. The au Jibun Bank Japan Services PMI stood at 53.1 in January 2025, marginally up from 53.0 the previous month, indicating the highest level since last September, a preliminary reading showed. The au Jibun Bank Japan Composite PMI rose to 51.6 in January 2025, up from 51.1 the previous month, flash data showed.

Meanwhile, Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda commented on Friday that "from a long-term perspective, rising interest rates will help improve financial institutions' profits." He emphasized that the central bank is prepared to respond swiftly to market shifts, including through market operations if abnormal moves occur.

The Nikkei 225 Index dropped 0.5% to below 38,500, while the broader Topix Index fell 0.3% to 2,726 on Friday, marking a third consecutive session of declines. The Japanese yen fell past 150 per dollar on Friday, retreating from 11-week highs, even as hotter-than-expected inflation figures strengthened the case for a hawkish outlook on Bank of Japan monetary policy.

More on Japan

  • Japan’s core inflation rate surges to 16-month high of 3% in December

  • Bank of Japan raises policy rate to 0.5%, highest since 2008 amid sustained inflation and rising wages

  • Japan unexpectedly logs trade surplus, exports hits record high & imports also shows positive growth

  • BOJ's Dec summary raises expectations of rate hike soon; keeps policy rate steady at 0.25%

  • Japan’s Nov headline inflation rate rises to three-month high of 2.9%; core inflation tops forecast

Solaris Oilfield Infrastructure reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and Q2 Adjusted EBITDA outlook
data.description:
• Solaris Oilfield Infrastructure press release (NYSE:SEI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $96M (+51.7% Y/Y) beats by $2.03M.
  • Total Adjusted EBITDA of $37 million.
  • Net cash from operating activities was $13 million in the fourth quarter 2024, and capital expenditures were approximately $127 million.
  • The company expects first quarter 2025 Total Adjusted EBITDA to be between $44 and $48 million and second quarter Total Adjusted EBITDA to be between $50 and $55 million.

data.title: Compañía de Minas Buenaventura GAAP EPS of $0.13 misses by $0.22, revenue of $299.6M beats by $2.88M
data.description:
• Compañía de Minas Buenaventura press release (NYSE:BVN): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.13 misses by $0.22.
  • Revenue of $299.6M (+18.0% Y/Y) beats by $2.88M.
  • 4Q24 EBITDA from direct operations was US$ 93.4 million, compared to US$ 74.0 million reported in 4Q23 excluding the November 2023 sale of Contacto Corredores De Seguros S.A., the Company’s risk and insurance brokerage.
  • Buenaventura ended the year with a cash position of US$ 478.4 million, with total net debt of US$ 148.3 million, resulting in a leverage ratio of 0.34x.
  • Buenaventura's 4Q24 consolidated silver production decreased by 0.4M Oz Y/Y, zinc production decreased by 60% YoY, and lead production decreased by 33% Y/Y.
  • Gold production decreased by 10% Y/Y due to decreased output at Orcopampa and Tambomayo. However, copper production increased by 5% YoY.
  • 4Q24 CAPEX related to San Gabriel was US$ 104.8 million

data.title: iRhythm targets $675M-$685M revenue for 2025 driven by Zio adoption and international expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Quentin Blackford highlighted a 24% year-over-year revenue growth in Q4 2024, driven by record demand and account openings for Zio products. He emphasized increased volume from primary care channels and national value-based care accounts as key contributors.

  • The company achieved significant milestones, including its first 1 million patients registered for Zio Monitor and crossing 2 million registrations through EHR-integrated accounts. Efforts to expand primary care adoption have led to over 50% of integrated delivery networks having at least one primary care physician prescribing Zio.

  • CFO Daniel Wilson reported Q4 revenue of $164.3 million and a gross margin of 70%, reflecting improved clinical operations and manufacturing efficiencies. Adjusted net income reached $0.2 million or $0.01 per share, a significant improvement from the prior year’s loss.

  • Expansion efforts in Europe and Japan were noted, with regulatory approval in Japan positioning the company for a mid-2025 commercial launch. Progress is also being made in Austria, the Netherlands, Switzerland, and Spain.

Outlook

  • The company reiterated its 2025 revenue guidance of $675 million to $685 million, driven by sustained U.S. volume growth, expanded primary care utilization, and international market contributions. International markets, including Japan, are expected to contribute approximately 1% of growth.

  • For Q1 2025, revenue is anticipated to represent about 22.5% of the full-year revenue, consistent with historical seasonality.

  • Adjusted EBITDA margin for 2025 is projected between 7% and 8%, including potential tariff impacts of 50 to 75 basis points.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue was $164.3 million, reflecting robust demand across Zio Monitor and Zio AT. Home enrollment accounted for approximately 22% of volume.

  • Gross margin improved to 70%, supported by sustainable operational efficiencies. Adjusted operating expenses were $116.7 million, influenced by innovation investments and FDA remediation activities.

  • Full-year 2025 guidance includes modest gross margin improvements, despite anticipated pricing headwinds and tariff impacts.

Q&A

  • Macauley Kilbane, William Blair: Asked about volume growth sustainability and guidance details. CFO Wilson highlighted balanced growth across Zio Monitor and Zio AT, with contributions from primary care channels and innovative partnerships.

  • Lilia-Celine Lozada, JPMorgan: Inquired about Q1 2025 trends. CEO Blackford cited continued momentum from Q4, driven by primary care and new national accounts, though noted some variability in innovative channel contributions.

  • Kallum Titchmarsh, Morgan Stanley: Asked about FDA remediation progress. CEO Blackford confirmed remediation efforts remain on track, with independent third-party audits expected in Q2 2025.

  • David Roman, Goldman Sachs: Sought clarification on the Epic integration’s volume impact. CEO Blackford noted early signs of volume uplift but emphasized the need for broader implementation before quantifying impact.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on guidance consistency and operational milestones, but raised concerns about FDA remediation and tariff impacts. Questions around MCT delays and integration challenges reflected a slightly skeptical tone.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing progress and strategic initiatives. Responses during Q&A were measured, acknowledging uncertainties in innovative channel contributions.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained consistent, with continued emphasis on addressing FDA concerns and driving operational efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated from 18.4% in Q3 to 24% in Q4 2024, signaling sustained demand across product lines.

  • Gross margin improved from 68.8% in Q3 to 70% in Q4, driven by operational efficiencies.

  • FDA remediation efforts progressed, with a clear timeline for completion by mid-2025. This marked a continuation of the company’s focus from Q3.

  • Analysts’ focus shifted from broader operational updates in Q3 to specific guidance-related inquiries in Q4, reflecting heightened scrutiny on execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified FDA remediation as a top priority, with $15 million in annual costs expected in 2025. Delays in MCT product submission were acknowledged but attributed to proactive adjustments.

  • Analysts questioned the impact of potential tariffs and pricing pressures on 2025 gross margin improvements.

  • Expansion into international markets, while promising, introduces risks related to regulatory approvals and market penetration timelines.

Final Takeaway

iRhythm Technologies reported strong Q4 2024 results, driven by expanding Zio adoption and operational improvements. The company remains focused on FDA remediation and international expansion, with strong revenue growth projected for 2025. While challenges such as potential tariffs and MCT delays remain, management’s proactive measures and strategic initiatives position the company for continued growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
9:45 AM PMI Composite Flash

The flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) provides an early estimate of current private sector business activity by combining information obtained from surveys of around 1,000 manufacturing and service sector companies. The flash data are released around ten days ahead of the final report and are typically based upon around 85 percent of the full survey sample.

10:00 AM Existing Home Sales

Existing home sales tally the number of previously constructed homes, condominiums and co-ops in which a sale closed during the month. Existing homes account for a larger share of the market than new homes and indicate housing market trends.

Sales are seen down a bit at an annual rate of 4.16 million units in January after rising more than expected to 4.24 million in December from 4.150 million in November.

10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment

The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.

Sentiment dropped a lot more than expected to 67.8 in the preliminary reading for February from 71.1 in January as inflation fears materialized in response to the threat of tariffs. The final reading for February is not expected to change much at 68.0.

10:00 AM Quarterly Services Survey.

The Census Bureau quarterly services survey focuses on information and technology-related service industries. These include information; professional, scientific and technical services; administrative & support services; and waste management and remediation services.

11:30 AM Fed's Jefferson Speech

Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson will speak on"Central Bank Communication" before the Macroeconomics and Monetary Policy Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco.

11:30 AM Fed's Daly Speech

San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will give opening remarks before the Macroeconomics and Monetary Policy Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. Changes in rig counts point to changes in the supply of oil & gas. The higher the rig count, the greater the upward pressure is on oil & gas supply and in turn the greater the downward pressure is on oil & gas prices.
